your_book:
Thursday handed Jaleel's book a strong session — nearly every holding finished green on a pre-holiday tape. The account sits at $20,697.98 with $158.01 in unrealized gains (+0.77%). The day's standout movers were Vertex Pharma (+6.03%), Samsara (+5.12%), Boston Scientific (+4.83%), Gilead (+4.21%), and Adobe (+4.14%). Adobe is the most important of those: it ripped +4.14% on the day yet remains -10.4% in the hole on Jaleel's cost basis — the largest single weight at 16.6% and $3,442. The stock is still deep underwater, and one good Thursday doesn't dissolve the AI-disruption narrative, the CFO departure, or the ARR de-emphasis story flagged at the June 11 print. The session pop is welcome; it is not vindication.

The drift problem is concentrated in the names where price and thesis are pulling apart. Boston Scientific (+4.83% Thursday, $1,128) carries a guidance slash — FY26 growth cut from 10.5% to 7–8.5% — with next earnings July 29 approaching fast; the single-day bounce does not change that setup. Zoetis (+3.24%) is -5.0% on cost with a STRONG BUY verdict and a QARP of 84.6 — the fundamentals haven't broken, but price hasn't caught up. Lululemon (+1.74%) is -7.4% on cost with Americas comps negative for a fifth straight quarter and tariff pressure on margins; the thesis is under genuine strain. NVIDIA was the lone red name (-1.39%), flagged for export controls and Senate scrutiny, with earnings not until August 25.

Rocket Lab ($1,305.98, 6.3% weight) is the structural concern that gets quieter attention than it deserves: rated AVOID with a QARP of 37.0 and still -4.8% on cost. It barely moved Thursday (+0.39%) and the rating hasn't improved. That weight sitting in an AVOID-rated name is the sharpest misalignment between verdict and allocation in the book. Keep an eye on the KO option sweep flagged in headlines — large call activity near the ask ahead of July 28 earnings is worth monitoring, though the $169 position is a small anchor, not a driver.

attention.headline: 6.3% weight in an AVOID-rated, sub-40 QARP name
attention.note: Rocket Lab carries a 37.0 QARP and an AVOID verdict while consuming $1,305.98 (6.3% of the book) — the largest misalignment between rating and allocation in the portfolio, and it demands an explicit hold or exit decision.

attention.headline: Largest position still -10.4% with narrative risk intact
attention.note: Thursday's +4.14% is encouraging but Adobe remains $3,442 deep in the red at -10.4%; the AI-moat fear, CFO exit, and ARR de-emphasis story haven't resolved, and the next catalyst isn't until September 10.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed; July 29 earnings closing fast
attention.note: Boston Scientific's FY26 growth was cut to 7–8.5% with management guiding flat through Q3 — Thursday's bounce doesn't change those numbers, and earnings arrive July 29.

attention.headline: Fifth straight negative Americas comp quarter; thesis under strain
attention.note: Lululemon is -7.4% on cost with a guidance cut, a tariff margin hit, and stalled China — the BUY verdict needs to be stress-tested against whether the recovery timeline still holds ahead of the ~September 4 next report.
